$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/types.h> (POSIX) — the base system typedefs, as language-NEUTRAL aliases injected by the semantic phase (see README + stdint.json). FIRST shipped descriptor in a SUBDIRECTORY: `#include <sys/types.h>` maps to `sys/types.json` (resolveSystemDescriptor preserves the subdir), DISTINCT from a top-level `<time.h>` -> `time.json` (so the later `<sys/time.h>` -> `sys/time.json` cannot collide). Widths are the LP64 model shared by the runnable unix targets (linux x86-64 + arm64): off_t/ssize_t/blksize_t/blkcnt_t/suseconds_t = the C `long` (i64); dev_t/ino_t/nlink_t = unsigned long (u64); mode_t/uid_t/gid_t/id_t/useconds_t = unsigned int (u32); pid_t/key_t = int (i32). These are the EXACT glibc x86-64 + arm64 (LP64, identical) widths the `struct stat`/`struct flock`/`struct timeval` layouts (later cycles) depend on, and they are the RUN-GREEN target (linux). macOS differs for several (mode_t = u16, dev_t = i32, nlink_t = u16, blksize_t = i32; off_t/ssize_t/uid_t/gid_t/pid_t/ino_t agree) — a descriptor baking a single platform's widths, deferred D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH (the same class as the stdint/stddef ILP32 deferral). It is INERT for the self-contained `shipped_sys_types` corpus (scalar `mode_t = 0` etc. — the width never affects the result); it FIRST bites at the FFI boundary (the macOS `struct stat` field layout, a later cycle), where a per-target width resolution closes it. time_t IS re-provided here with per-dataModel spelling arms (long on LP64, long long on LLP64) BYTE-identical to <time.h>'s time_t — the F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict consistency check (D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY) enforces that every descriptor declaring a shared typedef resolves it identically per target ( the first-wins `injectedNames` dedup makes the multi-header provision benign — a TU including both silently takes the first-read) because sqlite test_quota.c reaches time_t THROUGH <sys/types.h> and would otherwise fail; clock_t stays owned by <time.h> alone. ★ D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY (2026-07-20): a vocabulary TAG (`i64 "long"`) names a C type whose WIDTH is a property of the DATA MODEL, so a FLAT tag is only legal on a descriptor whose formats all share one model. This file ships on elf+macho (LP64) AND pe (LLP64), so `ssize_t` gained per-format arms exactly like its `off_t` sibling: LP64 spells it `long` (glibc/Darwin `__ssize_t`), while on LLP64 `long` is 32-BIT and the 64-bit signed size type is `long long` (MSVC's `SSIZE_T` is `__int64`). A flat `i64 "long"` here was a PHANTOM on pe — the pair (I64,"long") is unproducible under LLP64, so `_Generic(aSsizeT, long long:, long:)` matched NEITHER arm and `long long *p = aSsizeTPtr;` failed S0003. The invariant — a tag must have the width the LANGUAGE gives that name under the SELECTED data model — is now MACHINE-CHECKED by `checkShippedTypeConsistency` / tests/ffi/test_shipped_type_consistency.cpp, which enumerates every descriptor across every shipped target. No `library` (typedefs have no link surface); no `symbols`/`constants`/`macros`.
